CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Squar Milner Llp'S Request And Application For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Franck LeClerc, an individual, 6 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude lane, LLC, a California limited liability Company, Cafe Claude Restaurant, Inc., a California corporation, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, LLC, a California limited liability company, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, Inc., a California corporation and 2120 Greenwich, Inc., a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Jeffrey S. Ross. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Madison S. Spach Jr., Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff Squar Milner LLP, a California limited liability partnership.
Robert Daoro, Chief Financial Officer for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Daoro appeared for this morning's proceedings via CourtCall.
No appearance by defendants Franck LeClerc, et al.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Quantum Meruit, Open Book and Account Stated was filed on January 22, 2019, and personally served on defendants 6 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude lane, LLC, a California limited liability Company, Cafe Claude Restaurant, Inc., a California corporation, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, LLC, a California limited liability company, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, Inc., a California corporation and 2120 Greenwich, Inc., a California corporation, on February 4, 2019.
Plaintiff's Summons & Complaint were personally served on defendant Franck LeClerc, an individual, on March 11, 2019.
Defaults as to the seven entity defendants were entered on April 2, 2019.
Default as to defendant Franck LeClerc, an individual, was entered on May 6, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on May 17, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Squar Milner LLP, a California limited liability partnership, and against the defaulted defendants Franck LeClerc, an individual, 6 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude Lane, Inc., a California corporation, 8 Claude lane, LLC, a California limited liability Company, Cafe Claude Restaurant, Inc., a California corporation, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, LLC, a California limited liability company, Gitane Restaurant and Bar, Inc., a California corporation and 2120 Greenwich, Inc., a California corporation, Jointly and Severally, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $70,764.42.
Prejudgment Interest - $10,833.42.
Costs - $620.00.

Total - $82,217.84.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Continued To Aug-14-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Madison S. Spach, Jr., esq. (949-852-0710) of Spach, Capaldi & Waggaman LLP for Plaintiff via court call.
Guadalupe Gamino, esq. (415-706-2090) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs FRANCK LECLERC and CAFE CLAUDE RESTAURANT INC's Motion To Set Aside Default

Defendants Franck LeClerc and Cafe Claude Restaurant, Inc.'s motion to set aside entry of default is denied. Mr. LeClerc does not dispute that he was properly served with the summons and complaint, and he admits he received the March 6, 2019 letter from Plaintiff's counsel notifying him that Plaintiff would seek entry of default unless he responded to the complaint. Mr. LeClerc contends that he did not understand that he was required to respond to the complaint because the package also included a notice of an initial case management conference, but he does not explain why he (apparently) did not read the summons. Mr. LeClerc also offers no explanation for neglecting to forward the summons and complaint, which he acknowledges he recognized as legal papers, to his corporate counsel. Further, although he acknowledges receiving and signing for the letter sent to him by Plaintiff's counsel with return receipt requested, he asserts that he must have misplaced it without reading it. On this record, Defendants have failed to carry their burden of demonstrating "a satisfactory excuse for not responding to the original complaint in a timely manner." (Stiles v. Wallis (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1143, 1148-1149 [defendant did not show excusable neglect, where the notice on the face of the summons "was calculated to inform anyone capable of reading English . . . of the necessity of filing an answer with the court"]; see Jackson v. Bank of America (1983) 141 Cal.App.3d 55, 58 ["the acts which brought about the default must have been the acts of a reasonably prudent person under the same circumstances"].)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
